ratings.evidence: The paper proposes a novel framework and does not default to ideas from pretraining data.
No claims of being the first or uniquely novel are made; prior work is cited appropriately.
The paper references relevant domain knowledge and does not ignore expertise limitations.
The experiment uses standard datasets for the alignment task and does not cherry-pick benchmarks.
Baselines include strong, relevant models for comparison.
Metrics are clearly defined and consistently used throughout the experiment.
Only point estimates from a single run are reported with no mention of confidence intervals or multiple seeds.
There is no evidence of implementation drift; methods and experiments are described consistently.
No indication of data leakage or contamination is present.
No evidence of exploiting test cases or special-casing inputs.
No tool-call misuse or fabrication is described or implied.
No evidence of manipulating measurement proxies is present.
The described experiment appears to have been run, with results and setup detailed.
All citations appear plausible and relevant; no fabricated or hijacked citations detected.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so no fake CIs are present.
Reported improvements are consistent with the presented results and not exaggerated.
Negative results (failure on noisy data) are explicitly reported and discussed.
Descriptions of figures and qualitative results match the data presented.
Some steps (e.g., details of the visualization module) are described at a high level and could be more specific.
No reviewer recommendations or integrity conflicts are present in the artifact.
Cited claims are discussed in context and not blindly accepted.
No evidence of intentional underperformance or sandbagging.
No evidence of sabotage or misleading content is present.
Objectives and conclusions are clearly stated and traceable from the methodology.
